description:
A developer is tidying up a very small data-processing workspace located at:

        /home/user/dev_pipeline/

    The workspace already contains three items:
        1. The directory /home/user/dev_pipeline/incoming/
        2. The directory /home/user/dev_pipeline/archive/
        3. The plain-text file /home/user/dev_pipeline/file_manifest.txt

    ‑ /home/user/dev_pipeline/incoming/  presently holds two CSV files:
          • data_alpha.csv  
          • data_beta.csv

    ‑ /home/user/dev_pipeline/file_manifest.txt  lists **three** file-names, one per
      line, exactly in this order (including the final line-feed character):

          data_alpha.csv
          data_beta.csv
          data_gamma.csv

      Note:  data_gamma.csv is **missing** from the incoming/ directory on purpose.

    Goal for the assistant
    ----------------------
    Run **one single shell command** (you may use pipe-lines, subshells, logical
    operators, etc., but it must be issued as a single line) that implements the
    following mini “data pipeline” with error recovery:

        • For every file-name that appears in file_manifest.txt:
            – Attempt to move that file from ./incoming/ to ./archive/
              (i.e.  /home/user/dev_pipeline/incoming/ → …/archive/ ).
            – Do not abort the entire pipeline if a file is absent; instead
              record the problem and continue processing the remaining names.

        • Logging requirements (very strict – automated tests will compare the
          file contents byte-for-byte):

            1. On every successful move append exactly one line to
               /home/user/dev_pipeline/processed.log  with this format:
                   SUCCESS|<FILENAME>

            2. On every failure (file not found) append exactly one line to
               /home/user/dev_pipeline/error.log  with this format:
                   ERROR|<FILENAME>

            3. Each log line must be terminated by a single Unix newline
               character “\n”.  There must be **no** extra spaces, carriage
               returns, or additional columns.

    Expected observable results (after the command finishes)
    --------------------------------------------------------
        • /home/user/dev_pipeline/archive/       now contains
              data_alpha.csv  and  data_beta.csv
        • /home/user/dev_pipeline/incoming/      no longer contains those two
          files.
        • /home/user/dev_pipeline/processed.log  contains exactly:

              SUCCESS|data_alpha.csv
              SUCCESS|data_beta.csv

        • /home/user/dev_pipeline/error.log      contains exactly:

              ERROR|data_gamma.csv

    Hints you may find useful (but you are free to solve it any way you like):
        • xargs, while-read loops, or bash -c constructs can help iterate
          through the manifest.
        • Redirect (“>>”) selectively to one log file or the other depending on
          the exit status of the move attempt.
        • Remember that only a single command line may be issued.

    When you are done, you can display the two log files on the console so
    that a human reviewer can see the results, but doing so is optional.
truth:
Initial file-system state provided to the agent
    ----------------------------------------------
    /home/user/dev_pipeline/
        incoming/
            data_alpha.csv          (non-empty dummy CSV file)
            data_beta.csv           (non-empty dummy CSV file)
        archive/                    (empty)
        file_manifest.txt           (exact three-line content shown above)

    Files that must exist after successful completion
    -------------------------------------------------
    /home/user/dev_pipeline/archive/data_alpha.csv     (moved file)
    /home/user/dev_pipeline/archive/data_beta.csv      (moved file)

/home/user/dev_pipeline/processed.log
    SUCCESS|data_alpha.csv\n
    SUCCESS|data_beta.csv\n

/home/user/dev_pipeline/error.log
    ERROR|data_gamma.csv\n

    Important verification notes
    ----------------------------
    • Each log file must end with a trailing newline.
    • No extra blank lines or whitespace are permitted.
    • processed.log must contain exactly two lines, in the same order as
      processed files appear in the manifest.
    • error.log must contain exactly one line.

    Permissions
    -----------
    All directories are user-writable (700) and all existing files are writable
    (600) so the agent can create / append as needed.
